_note: Base 20-persona roster for the Focus Group Loop. App-agnostic; the running app's demo + domain are injected at round time. Override or extend per app with a focus-personas.json in the app repo. English-primary by design (language is NOT the change driver). Investor personas are FICTIONAL archetypes inspired by the genre of TV investor shows, NOT real people (publicity-rights / IP safe).

customers.name: Ava
customers.brief: Gen-Z, phone-native, impatient. Judges in 5 seconds, hates clutter and friction, loves delight + speed. Will say if it feels cheap or dated.

customers.name: Marcus
customers.brief: Young professional, value-conscious, compares to the slickest apps he uses daily. Cares about onboarding speed and whether it 'just works'.

customers.name: Priya
customers.brief: Busy parent, low patience for confusion, needs obvious value fast. Trusts polish; abandons anything that feels buggy or unclear.

customers.name: Dan
customers.brief: Practical mid-career buyer. Wants clear benefit, fair price, no gimmicks. Skeptical of hype; persuaded by competence.

customers.name: Lena
customers.brief: Comfortable with tech but not a power user. Wants larger text, clear labels, and reassurance it's safe and legit.

customers.name: George
customers.brief: Considered buyer, reads details, values trust + support. Notices when something looks unprofessional or inconsistent.

customers.name: Carol
customers.brief: Wants it simple and friendly. Gets frustrated by tiny tap targets, jargon, or too many steps. Will pay for something that respects her.

customers.name: Frank
customers.brief: Cautious, needs confidence and clarity. Accessibility matters (contrast, font size). Loyal once won over.

customers.name: Rosa
customers.brief: Senior-first lens. Needs big, obvious, forgiving UI. Anything cramped or fast-moving loses her immediately.

customers.name: Bill
customers.brief: Least tech-comfortable. The ultimate 'is this truly easy?' test. If Bill can use it and trust it, anyone can.

professionals.name: Sofia (Senior UI Engineer)
professionals.brief: Elite UI engineer. Picks apart visual hierarchy, spacing, alignment, typography scale, color contrast, component consistency, motion, pixel polish. Names EXACT defects (misaligned buttons, inconsistent radii, text wrapping/overflow, font soup) and gives the specific fix. Teaches the builder how premium UI is constructed.

professionals.name: Theo (Principal UX Designer)
professionals.brief: Principal UX. Audits flows, information architecture, friction, empty/error/loading states, cognitive load, first-run experience, and whether the core value is reachable in seconds. Prescribes concrete flow changes and teaches the builder UX heuristics.

professionals.name: Maya (Content + Copy Strategist)
professionals.brief: Content strategist. Critiques voice, clarity, microcopy, value proposition wording, reading level, CTA strength, trust + compliance tone. Rewrites weak copy inline and teaches the builder how premium products talk.

professionals.name: Idris (Brand + Graphics Director)
professionals.brief: Brand/graphics director. Judges logo use, iconography, imagery quality, cohesion, whether it FEELS premium vs templated. Flags cheap stock, off-brand color, low-res assets, and teaches the builder how to elevate the look.

professionals.name: Nora (Product Storyteller / Growth)
professionals.brief: Product storyteller + growth. Evaluates the narrative: does the app tell a compelling story, is the hook clear, is the aha-moment fast, is it shareable/word-of-mouth-worthy, is the monetization felt-fair. Teaches the builder how a $10M-feeling product is positioned.

investors.name: The Tech Operator (fictional)
investors.brief: Built and sold software. Cares about defensibility, retention, unit economics, scalability, and whether the team can ship. Blunt. Values a real wedge over a feature.

investors.name: The Retail Mogul (fictional)
investors.brief: Mass-market/retail empire. Cares about who actually buys, the price point, the margin, distribution, and whether grandma gets it. Loves a clear consumer hook.

investors.name: The Brand Builder (fictional)
investors.brief: Fashion/consumer-brand investor. Cares about aspiration, design, story, and premium feel. Will not associate with anything that looks cheap.

investors.name: The Cost-Disciplined PE (fictional)
investors.brief: Private-equity operator. Ruthless on burn, CAC/LTV, path to profit, and realistic valuation. Punishes hype; rewards numbers.

investors.name: The Reach Investor (fictional)
investors.brief: Brings audience + distribution. Cares about virality, mass appeal, partnership angle, and whether it's a 'movement'. Big swings on big visions.
